userStories.title: View Books in Paginated Table
userStories.description: As a library administrator, I want to see all books in a paginated table format so that I can quickly scan the collection and manage inventory efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Table displays 6 columns: Cover (thumbnail), Title, Author, Genre, Year, Actions
Cover images display as 50x75px thumbnails
Missing covers show placeholder book icon
Table displays 4 books per page by default
Pagination shows 'Showing X-Y of Z books'
Previous and Next buttons for navigation
Page numbers clickable for direct access
Current page highlighted with blue background
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books?page=0&size=4, React Table component, skeleton loading states

userStories.title: Sort Books by Column
userStories.description: As a catalog manager, I want to sort books by clicking column headers so that I can organize the view by title, author, genre, or year.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking column header sorts ascending
Clicking again sorts descending
Sort indicator (▲/▼) shows current sort direction
Sortable columns: Title, Author, Genre, Year
Sort persists across pagination
Default sort: Title ascending
userStories.technicalNotes: Client-side sorting for current page, server-side sorting with query param sortBy

userStories.title: Filter Books by Genre
userStories.description: As a catalog manager, I want to filter books by genre using dropdown so that I can view specific categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Genre dropdown displays all available genres in collection
Dropdown includes 'All Genres' default option
Selecting genre immediately filters table
Filtered results update pagination count
Filter persists across pagination
Clear filter by selecting 'All Genres'
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books?genre=Fiction, populate dropdown from distinct genres in database

userStories.title: Filter Books by Author and Year
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter books by author and publication year so that I can narrow down the collection.
acceptanceCriteria: Author dropdown populated with all unique authors
Year dropdown populated with all unique publication years (descending)
Both dropdowns include 'All' default option
Multiple filters work together (AND logic)
Example: Genre=Fiction AND Author=Orwell shows only Orwell's fiction
Filters update pagination and result count
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books?genre=Fiction&author=Orwell&year=1949

userStories.title: Quick Search from Header
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly search for books from the header search bar so that I can find books without navigating to a dedicated search page.
acceptanceCriteria: Search bar visible in header on all screens
Placeholder text: 'Search books...'
Search executes as user types (debounced 300ms)
Matches title, author, ISBN (partial, case-insensitive)
Results filter table in real-time
Clear 'X' button appears when search has text
Empty results show 'No books found' message
Search works in combination with dropdown filters
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books?search=gatsby, debounce using lodash or custom hook

userStories.title: Navigate to Book from Table Row
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click on a book row to view its details so that I can see comprehensive information.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking anywhere on table row (except action buttons) navigates to Book Details
Row shows hover state (background color change) to indicate clickability
Cursor changes to pointer on row hover
Navigation preserves back button functionality
Back button from details returns to list with preserved filters/search/page
userStories.technicalNotes: React Router Link wrapper on table row, onClick handler on row

userStories.title: Add New Book with Required Fields
userStories.description: As a library administrator, I want to add new books with required metadata so that the catalog has complete and accurate information.
acceptanceCriteria: Form displays with 2-column layout (desktop)
Required fields marked with red asterisk (*): Title, ISBN, Author, Genre, Year, Language
ISBN validates format: ISBN-10 (10 digits) or ISBN-13 (13 digits) with optional hyphens
ISBN uniqueness checked against database on blur
Genre dropdown: Fiction, Non-Fiction, Dystopian, Romance, Mystery, Science, Biography, Fantasy
Language dropdown: English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic
Year validates: integer between 1000 and current year
Error messages display below fields on blur or submit attempt
Submit button disabled during API call, shows 'Saving...'
On success: Navigate to Books List, show success toast 'Book added successfully!'
On error: Show error toast with message, remain on form
userStories.technicalNotes: POST /api/v1/books, React Hook Form + Zod validation, axios for API

userStories.title: Add Book with Optional Fields
userStories.description: As a library administrator, I want to provide additional book details when available so that the catalog is as complete as possible.
acceptanceCriteria: Optional fields: Publisher, Pages, Price, Stock Quantity, Description
Publisher: max 100 characters
Pages: positive integer, validated on blur
Price: decimal (10,2), validated >= 0, currency format $X.XX
Stock Quantity: integer >= 0, default 0
Description: textarea, max 5000 characters
Optional fields validated only if provided
Form can be submitted with only required fields
userStories.technicalNotes: Same form as US-007, conditional validation in Zod schema

userStories.title: Upload Book Cover Image
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload book cover images so that books are visually identifiable in the catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: Large upload area with dashed border and upload icon
Text: 'Click to upload or drag and drop'
Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP
Maximum file size: 5MB
Drag-drop support for image files
Image preview displays immediately after upload
'Remove' button appears after upload to clear image
File validation shows error toast for invalid type/size
Image uploaded separately from form submission
Form can be submitted without cover image (optional)
userStories.technicalNotes: File input with drag-drop, POST /api/v1/books/{id}/cover for image upload, store URL in coverImageUrl field

userStories.title: Cancel Add Book
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cancel adding a book so that I can return to the list without saving incomplete data.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Cancel' button displayed at bottom-left of form (gray)
'← Back' button in top-right header
Both buttons navigate to Books List View
No data saved to database on cancel
No confirmation dialog shown (data loss expected)
Uploaded cover image discarded
userStories.technicalNotes: React Router navigate, no API call

userStories.title: Quick Search with Single Input
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search quickly with a single search box so that I can find books without filling multiple fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Large search input at top of Search Books screen
Placeholder: 'Search by title, author, ISBN, or keyword...'
Search icon displayed in input
Green 'Search' button executes search
Enter key also executes search
Matches title, author, ISBN (partial, case-insensitive)
Results displayed in Books List View with pagination
Search term preserved in URL for bookmarking
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/search?q=gatsby, redirect to Books List with results

userStories.title: Advanced Search by Text Fields
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search by specific fields so that I can narrow results precisely.
acceptanceCriteria: Advanced Search section below Quick Search
Text inputs: Title, Author, ISBN, Publisher
All text searches use partial match, case-insensitive
Multiple filled fields use AND logic
Example: Title='Gatsby' AND Author='Fitzgerald' returns only matching books
Empty fields ignored in search
Search Tips banner explains usage
Results paginated
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/search?title=gatsby&author=fitzgerald

userStories.title: Advanced Search by Dropdowns
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter search results by categorical fields so that I can find books in specific categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Dropdown filters: Genre, Language, Availability
Genre: Same 8 options as Add Book form
Language: Same 10 options as Add Book form
Availability: All, Available (In Stock), Out of Stock
All dropdowns optional
Filters use exact match
Work in combination with text searches
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/search?genre=Fiction&language=English&availability=true

userStories.title: Advanced Search by Date and Price Ranges
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search for books within date and price ranges so that I can find books from specific eras or price points.
acceptanceCriteria: Year range: From (YYYY) and To (YYYY) inputs
Price range: Min ($) and Max ($) inputs
Both ranges optional
If only From provided: year >= From
If only To provided: year <= To
If both provided: year BETWEEN From AND To
Same logic for price range
Validation: From <= To
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/search?yearFrom=1900&yearTo=2000&minPrice=10&maxPrice=50

userStories.title: Sort Search Results
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sort search results so that I can view books in my preferred order.
acceptanceCriteria: Sort Results By dropdown with 8 options
Options: Relevance, Title (A-Z), Author (A-Z), Year (Newest First), Year (Oldest First), Price (Low to High), Price (High to Low), Rating (Highest First)
Default: Relevance for Quick Search, Title (A-Z) for Advanced Search
Sort applies to results before display
Sort persists across pagination
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/search?sortBy=year-desc

userStories.title: Search with Boolean Filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want additional boolean filters so that I can find books meeting specific criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkboxes: In Stock Only, On Sale, New Releases
In Stock Only: filters books where stockQuantity > 0
On Sale: filters books with discount (future feature)
New Releases: filters books where publicationYear >= currentYear - 1
All checkboxes unchecked by default
Multiple checkboxes use AND logic
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/search?inStockOnly=true&newReleases=true

userStories.title: Load Book for Editing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit book information so that I can correct errors or update details.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Edit button on Books List navigates to Edit Book Form
Form fetches book via GET /api/v1/books/{id}
Loading skeleton displayed while fetching
All form fields pre-populated with current book data
Book ID and Last Updated date shown in yellow banner at top
Format: 'Book ID: BK-2024-001234 | Last Updated: 2024-01-15'
Cover image preview shows current cover
'Change Image' button overlaid on cover
On fetch error (404): Show error toast and redirect to Books List
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/{id}, populate React Hook Form with setValue

userStories.title: ISBN Immutability in Edit Form
userStories.description: As a system, I want to prevent ISBN changes so that book identity remains consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: ISBN input field styled as read-only (grayed out)
Lock icon (🔒) displayed next to ISBN label
Cursor shows 'not-allowed' on hover
Tooltip on hover: 'ISBN cannot be changed after creation'
ISBN value visible but not editable
ISBN not included in update request payload
userStories.technicalNotes: disabled prop on input, CSS styling for read-only state

userStories.title: Update Book Fields
userStories.description: As a user, I want to change any book detail except ISBN so that I can keep information up-to-date.
acceptanceCriteria: All fields editable except ISBN, id, createdAt
Editable fields: title, author, publisher, genre, publicationYear, pages, language, price, stockQuantity, description, coverImage
Same validation rules as Add Book form
User can modify one or multiple fields
Changes not saved until 'Update Book' clicked
No changes detection: If no fields modified, show info toast 'No changes detected'
On success: Navigate to Book Details, show success toast 'Book updated successfully!'
System auto-updates: updatedAt = NOW(), isAvailable = (stockQuantity > 0)
userStories.technicalNotes: PUT /api/v1/books/{id}, compare form values with initial values

userStories.title: Change Book Cover in Edit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to update the book cover image so that I can replace incorrect or low-quality images.
acceptanceCriteria: Current cover displayed as preview (larger than table thumbnail)
'Change Image' button overlaid or adjacent to cover
Clicking button opens file picker
Selected image replaces preview immediately
Same validation as Add Book: format, size
User can keep existing cover or upload new one
New cover uploaded on form submission
userStories.technicalNotes: File input, preview with URL.createObjectURL, PUT with multipart/form-data

userStories.title: Cancel Book Edit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cancel editing so that I can discard unwanted changes.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Cancel' button at bottom-left (gray)
'← Back' button in top-right header
Clicking Cancel navigates to Book Details screen
Clicking Back navigates to Books List
No changes saved to database
No confirmation dialog (data loss expected)
userStories.technicalNotes: React Router navigate, no API call

userStories.title: View Comprehensive Book Details
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view all information about a book so that I can see comprehensive details in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Left column: Large cover image (~300x450px)
Availability badge overlaid in top-right of cover: 'In Stock' (green) or 'Out of Stock' (red)
Right column: Title (H1), Author (H2), Rating (stars), Price box, Details grid, Description
Title displayed prominently (24-28px font)
Author displayed as 'by {author name}'
Layout responsive: stacks vertically on mobile
Back button (← Back) in top-right returns to Books List
On load error (404): Show 'Book not found' and redirect to Books List
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/{id}, two-column flexbox/grid layout

userStories.title: Display Book Rating
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the title, author, and rating clearly so that I can quickly identify the book and its popularity.
acceptanceCriteria: Rating displayed as stars (★★★★☆) with numeric value
Format: '4.2 / 5.0 (1,234 reviews)'
Stars partially filled based on decimal rating
Example: 4.2 shows 4 full stars + partial 5th star
If rating is NULL: Display 'No ratings yet'
Review count formatted with comma separators
userStories.technicalNotes: Star component with fill percentage, CSS for partial stars

userStories.title: Display Book Price and Availability
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see pricing and availability so that I know if the book is in stock and affordable.
acceptanceCriteria: Price box with border and subtle background
Current price prominently displayed: '$15.99' (large, bold)
If original price differs: Show strikethrough original and discount %
Stock info: '{stockQuantity} copies available'
If stockQuantity = 0: 'Out of stock'
Price formatted with $ and 2 decimal places
userStories.technicalNotes: Conditional rendering for discount, number formatting

userStories.title: Display Book Metadata Grid
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all book metadata so that I have complete information.
acceptanceCriteria: Details displayed as label-value pairs in 2-column grid
Labels: ISBN, Publisher, Publication Year, Language, Pages, Genre
Values populated from book data
If value is NULL: Display '—' or 'Not specified'
Grid responsive: single column on mobile
Consistent spacing and typography
userStories.technicalNotes: CSS Grid or definition list (dl/dt/dd) for label-value pairs

userStories.title: Display Book Description
userStories.description: As a user, I want to read the book description so that I understand what the book is about.
acceptanceCriteria: Section header: 'Description'
Full description text with proper line breaks
If description is NULL or empty: 'No description available.'
Long descriptions support scrolling or expand/collapse (if >500 chars)
Text formatted with proper spacing
userStories.technicalNotes: Text block with white-space: pre-wrap for line breaks

userStories.title: Display Metadata Bar and Increment View Count
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see when the book was added and last updated so that I know data recency.
acceptanceCriteria: Gray background info bar at bottom of detail view
Format: 'Book ID: BK-2024-001234 | Added: 2023-12-10 | Last Updated: 2024-01-15 | Views: 3,456'
Dates formatted as YYYY-MM-DD
View count formatted with comma separators
View count automatically incremented on page load
Increment happens asynchronously (doesn't block display)
userStories.technicalNotes: PUT /api/v1/books/{id}/increment-view after initial load, useEffect with dependency

userStories.title: Action Buttons on Book Details
userStories.description: As a user, I want quick access to actions so that I can edit, duplicate, export, or delete books.
acceptanceCriteria: Four action buttons at bottom of screen in a row
Edit Book (blue #2196F3): Navigates to Edit Form
Duplicate (orange #FF9800): Creates copy and navigates to Edit Form
Export Data (gray #9E9E9E): Downloads book data
Delete Book (red #F44336): Shows delete confirmation modal
Buttons responsive: stack vertically on mobile
All buttons accessible via keyboard
userStories.technicalNotes: Button components with onClick handlers, React Router for navigation

userStories.title: Show Delete Confirmation Modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want to confirm deletion with book details shown so that I don't accidentally delete the wrong book.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Delete Book' button shows modal overlay
Modal centered on screen with semi-transparent black background
Modal title: '⚠ Confirm Delete' with warning icon
Modal body shows highlighted box with: Title: {title}, ISBN: {isbn}
Warning text: 'This action cannot be undone'
Two buttons: 'Cancel' (gray, left) and 'Delete Permanently' (red, right)
ESC key closes modal without deleting
Clicking outside modal closes without deleting
Focus trapped within modal for accessibility
userStories.technicalNotes: Modal component with overlay, React Portal, focus trap using react-focus-lock

userStories.title: Cancel Book Deletion
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cancel deletion if I change my mind so that the book is not deleted.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Cancel' button closes modal
No API request sent
User returned to Book Details view unchanged
ESC key also cancels
Clicking outside modal also cancels
userStories.technicalNotes: Modal state management, no API call

userStories.title: Permanently Delete Book
userStories.description: As a user, I want to permanently delete the book when I confirm so that it is removed from the catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Delete Permanently' sends DELETE request to /api/v1/books/{id}
Modal shows loading state: button disabled, spinner visible
On success (200/204): Show success toast 'Book deleted permanently'
On success: Navigate to Books List View
Deleted book no longer appears in list
Pagination updates to reflect new count
On error (404/500): Show error toast, close modal
Hard delete: Book row removed from database permanently
userStories.technicalNotes: DELETE /api/v1/books/{id}, navigate on success

userStories.title: Duplicate Book for Quick Entry
userStories.description: As a data entry clerk, I want to duplicate books so that I can quickly add similar books with minor changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Orange 'Duplicate' button on Book Details screen
Clicking Duplicate sends POST to /api/v1/books/{id}/duplicate
Backend creates copy with all fields from original
Title appended with ' (Copy)' suffix
ISBN field cleared (must be unique)
New id, createdAt, updatedAt generated
viewCount, reviewCount, rating reset to 0/NULL
Cover image copied
User navigated to Edit Book Form with duplicated book ID
Edit form pre-populated with duplicated data
User must provide new unique ISBN before saving
Cancel deletes the duplicate without saving
userStories.technicalNotes: POST /api/v1/books/{id}/duplicate returns new book ID, navigate to /books/{newId}/edit

userStories.title: Export Book Data as JSON
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export book data in JSON format so that I can use it in external systems or create backups.
acceptanceCriteria: Gray 'Export Data' button on Book Details screen
Clicking button triggers file download (default JSON format)
JSON contains complete book object with all 18 fields
Filename format: book_{isbn}_{date}.json
File downloaded to user's default download location
JSON is well-formatted and valid
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/{id}/export?format=json, Content-Disposition header for download

userStories.title: Export Book Data as CSV and PDF
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export book data in CSV or PDF format so that I can use the data in spreadsheets or printable documents.
acceptanceCriteria: Format selection dropdown or query parameter support
CSV format: Single-row CSV with headers for all 18 fields
CSV suitable for spreadsheet import (Excel, Google Sheets)
PDF format: Formatted document with cover image and all details
PDF includes book cover, title, author, all metadata, description
Filename format: book_{isbn}_{date}.{ext}
User can choose format before download
userStories.technicalNotes: GET /api/v1/books/{id}/export?format=csv|pdf, use library like Apache POI (CSV) or iText (PDF)

userStories.title: Responsive Layout for Mobile Devices
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to use the app on my phone so that I can manage books on the go.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile (<768px): Single column forms, card layout for book list
Header: Logo + hamburger menu (search and action buttons in menu)
Books List: Convert table to card layout (cover, title, author, action buttons)
Filter dropdowns: Stack vertically
Forms: Single column layout, full-width inputs
Book Details: Stack cover and info vertically
Touch targets minimum 44x44px
Text remains readable (min 16px for body text)
Images scale appropriately without distortion
Tested on iPhone and Android devices
userStories.technicalNotes: Tailwind CSS responsive utilities (sm:, md:, lg:), mobile-first approach

userStories.title: Responsive Layout for Tablet Devices
userStories.description: As a tablet user, I want an optimized layout so that I can use the app comfortably on my tablet.
acceptanceCriteria: Tablet (768-1024px): 2-column forms maintained
Header: Logo + search + action button (search bar width reduced)
Books List: Scrollable table horizontally or reduce columns to fit
Filter dropdowns: 3 columns
Book Details: 2-column layout maintained
Touch-friendly button sizes
Tested on iPad and Android tablets
userStories.technicalNotes: Tailwind CSS md: breakpoint utilities

userStories.title: Loading States for Tables and Forms
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see loading indicators so that I know the system is processing my request.
acceptanceCriteria: Tables: Skeleton loaders show placeholder rows while fetching
Forms: Spinner displayed while fetching book data for edit
Buttons: Disabled during submission with loading spinner
Button text changes: 'Add Book' → 'Saving...', 'Update Book' → 'Updating...', 'Delete' → 'Deleting...'
Loading overlay dims background during long operations
No double-submit possible while loading
Loading states accessible to screen readers (aria-busy)
userStories.technicalNotes: Skeleton component, button disabled prop, loading state in React state

userStories.title: Success and Error Toast Notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want success and error messages so that I know my action completed or what went wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: Success toasts: 'Book added successfully!', 'Book updated successfully!', 'Book deleted permanently'
Error toasts: Specific messages for validation errors, network errors, server errors
Toasts appear in top-right corner
Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds
Manual dismiss via X button
Green background for success, red for error
Toast announced to screen readers (aria-live)
Multiple toasts stack vertically
userStories.technicalNotes: Toast library (react-hot-toast or custom), context provider for global toasts

userStories.title: Empty States with Helpful Messages
userStories.description: As a user, I want helpful messages when no data is available so that I know what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty collection: 'No books yet. Add your first book!' with prominent + Add Book button
Empty search: 'No books found. Try different search terms.' with 'Clear search' link
Empty filter: 'No books match your filters. Try clearing filters.' with 'Clear filters' button
Empty states include relevant icon or illustration
Empty states provide actionable next steps
Centered vertically and horizontally in content area
userStories.technicalNotes: Conditional rendering based on data length, empty state component

userStories.title: Keyboard Navigation Support
userStories.description: As a keyboard-only user, I want to navigate the entire app with keyboard so that I can use it without a mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements accessible via Tab key
Tab order follows logical visual order
Focus trap in modal dialogs (tab cycles within modal)
ESC key closes modals and dropdowns
Enter key submits forms and activates buttons
Arrow keys navigate dropdown options
Space key toggles checkboxes
Clear focus indicators on all interactive elements (visible outline)
Skip to main content link at top for keyboard users
userStories.technicalNotes: tabindex, onKeyDown handlers, CSS :focus-visible, focus trap library

userStories.title: Screen Reader Support
userStories.description: As a screen reader user, I want proper labels and announcements so that I understand the interface.
acceptanceCriteria: ARIA labels for all form inputs (aria-label or aria-labelledby)
ARIA role='dialog' for modals with aria-modal='true'
ARIA live regions for toast notifications (aria-live='polite' for success, 'assertive' for errors)
Alt text for all book cover images (title + author)
Form validation errors announced (aria-describedby linking to error messages)
Button states announced (disabled, loading)
Semantic HTML: header, nav, main, footer, article, section
Landmark regions for navigation (role='navigation', role='main')
Passes screen reader testing (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
userStories.technicalNotes: ARIA attributes, semantic HTML, testing with screen readers

userStories.title: Color Contrast Compliance
userStories.description: As a visually impaired user, I want sufficient color contrast so that I can read text easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Color contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 for normal text
Color contrast ratio >= 3:1 for large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold)
Form validation errors use red + icon (not color alone)
Links distinguishable by underline, not just color
Focus indicators high contrast (visible against all backgrounds)
Passes automated contrast testing (axe, Lighthouse)
Passes WCAG 2.1 Level AA requirements
userStories.technicalNotes: Color palette with sufficient contrast, contrast checking tools, Tailwind contrast utilities

userStories.title: Fast Page Load Times
userStories.description: As a user, I want pages to load quickly so that I don't waste time waiting.
acceptanceCriteria: Page load time <2 seconds for all screens (Time to Interactive)
First Contentful Paint (FCP) <1.5s
Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) <0.1
Images lazy loaded with progressive loading
Code splitting: Routes lazy loaded with React.lazy()
Bundle size optimized: main bundle <200KB gzipped
Lighthouse performance score >90
Tested on standard broadband (>10 Mbps)
userStories.technicalNotes: React.lazy + Suspense, Vite code splitting, image optimization, lazy loading library

userStories.title: Fast API Response Times
userStories.description: As a user, I want search results to appear instantly so that I can find books efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: API response time <500ms for 95% of requests (P95)
Search results returned <1 second for collections up to 10,000 books
Database indexes on frequently queried columns: title, author, genre, year, isbn
Composite indexes: (genre, year), (author, title)
Query optimization: No N+1 queries, use JOIN efficiently
Connection pooling configured: HikariCP with min 5, max 20 connections
Pagination limits result set: max 100 books per page
Debounced search input (300ms) reduces API calls
Response caching for static data (genres, languages)
userStories.technicalNotes: Database indexes in schema.sql, @Query optimization, Spring Cache, debounce hook

userStories.title: Edit Book from List
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit book details directly from the list so that I can quickly update information.
acceptanceCriteria: Blue 'Edit' button displayed in Actions column for each book
Clicking Edit navigates to Edit Book Form (/books/{id}/edit)
Edit form pre-populated with book's current data
After successful edit, user returned to Books List view
Edit button accessible via keyboard (Tab + Enter)
userStories.technicalNotes: React Router Link to /books/:id/edit

userStories.title: Delete Book from List
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete books from the list with confirmation so that I can remove obsolete books safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Red 'Delete' button displayed in Actions column for each book
Clicking Delete navigates to Book Details page with delete modal open
Modal shows confirmation as per US-029
On confirmation, book deleted and user returned to Books List
Delete button accessible via keyboard
userStories.technicalNotes: Navigate to /books/:id?action=delete, modal opens if query param present

userStories.title: Clear All Search Filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want to clear all search criteria so that I can start a new search without manually clearing each field.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Clear Filters' button displayed in Advanced Search form (gray)
Clicking button resets all text inputs to empty
Clicking button resets all dropdowns to default/placeholder
Clicking button unchecks all checkboxes
No API call made on clear (local state reset only)
User can then enter new criteria or search to show all books
userStories.technicalNotes: Form reset() method, React Hook Form reset function
